review_text_raw: This firm is a high-volume settlement firm. Insurance companies know this, mediators know this, and as a result, your benefits are drastically minimized. They put a lien on your claim to keep you from leaving them for a better firm once you see how their business model is hurting your life outcome. You will be hurt by double taxation, as, unlike most other disability firms, they will not issue you a tax form directly. This firm says one thing on the phone, then denies it after the truth comes out. Anything you are relying on from them must be in writing, or it is just a manipulative ploy by their attorneys. Best advice: figure out which federal district court your case belongs in and hire a local firm in that jurisdiction with trial experience before the judges in that jurisdiction.

review_text_raw: This is an honest review from a former client of Dell Disability. I hired them to represent me in a STD appeal and to handle the LTD claim once the policy kicked in. Reliance Standard had provided me with a claim file that had my name at the top but the letter was for someone who worked for a different employer and had all of their claim information in the letter. I had not been able to get a copy of the policies and the claim file was just a bunch of compressed and cut off pages. I had no idea what a claim file was supposed to look like but Dell Disability does. Dell represented me for 240 days. If I did not remove them as counsel on 4/28/23 Reliance Standard would have denied the claim when they got the peer review back. I would have never known that Dell never started the LTD claim and when the STD claim was denied I would have had no recourse. I would have never known that Reliance Standard only saved 3 pages of medical out of 351 pages to their administrative record. They appeal that I was sent by Dell Disability wasnâ€™t the appeal that Reliance Standard. At the point I removed Dell i was owed 16 weeks of STD and the other 28 weeks would have been under the LTD policy. That is like Dell going to work for 43 weeks and only trying to get paid for 16. I think sometimes itâ€™s more important for the employer to get rid of the claim all together and that was attempted with my claim. They waited to even start the appeal until after the policy with my employer was terminated. I found enough proof (it took me 707 days) but I was paid for all 707 days and put back on claim for LTD benefits. My advice: read your contract before you sign it. The contract doesnâ€™t say a single thing Dell will do for you. Only what you are paying them and that all contact will go through Dell. You canâ€™t request to see what Reliance has saved to itâ€™s files yourself. That is intentional. Those records wonâ€™t be the same. Dell Disability is just setting the expectations in their videos of exactly what they are going to do.

review_text_raw: Thought was going well, welp they care about the money and not fighting . They really wouldn't argue anything. My divorce was finalized in Sept. I'm still paying over on child support because Sterling never filed paperwork. I emailed my attorney at the end of oct-nov, and was told it takes a couple months. Still pay more child support than necessary. I called child support they said nothing was filed about the reduction.. Called the attorneys office and assistant left a message with attorney about it. Never heard anything back. They didn't finish the case or file the paperwork. I am trying to work through this on.my own now. I called and emailed attorney with no response.. Absolutely terrible representation I received . I would not recommend to anyone. Follow up: Attorney Jeff Hughes responded to this.. I called his office, and sat on hold for a bit.. I was told they left a message for him.. I have heard nothing back from the office that says they take these matters seriously for a few days now. I wouldn't hire this law firm to fight a speeding ticket. Oh and I was able to get all the facts and findings from the courthouse to give to child support.. I'm still paying over on child support but hopefully soon it is mitigated.. I do not recommend this firm at all, and never will.. I feel.like I was taken advantage of, I feel like I was misrepresented, and feel.like I have taken a huge and unnecessary financial hit due to this law firms lack of representation.

review_text_raw: It took me a little over a month after firing Ginger at your family law center to actually leave this review. I wanted to make sure I was not saying anything out of anger but to be totally honest as to the experience i had. Communication was horrible. Even after making an appointment I was still unable to actually talk to ginger about my case. After a month of no updates and calling many times for an update, I finally got angry over the phone demanding an answer. When I learned ginger had hired a new lawyer who was also my ex's lawyer creating a conflict of interest in my case. I was also charged for every phone call trying to get an update for a month. The same day the receptionist informed me of this, I magically got an email from Ginger explaining the situation. To little to late. I hired Ginger in late January 2024 and fired her 9 months later when not even a single issue in my divorce was taken care of. I had already filed and served my husband before hiring Ginger. After firing Ginger I requested a copy of my file for my new lawyer when I saw that my file was sent I downloaded it. Upon opening the file it was not my file. But the files of many of her offices other clients including full tax returns, addresses, social security numbers, child information, etc. this same file was also cc'ed to my new lawyer whom I called and informed of the situation after informing your family law center of the mistake, and deleting all the files sent to me. I ended up hiring a lawyer who is half the price and way more effective. I was issued a court date about a month after hiring the new lawyer dispite him having to start from the beginning. While my divorce is not yet completed it is at least finally moving forward. In my experience you can find a better lawyer for a more reasonable price.

I would recommend Roberta in a divorce and/or custody proceeding should you find yourself in the unenviable position of experiencing one or both.
